NCT02446912.name: TULIP-1
NCT02446912.group: Moderate-severe SLE on standard therapy, anifrolumab 300 mg IV Q4W vs placebo (wk 52, BICLA)

allOutcomes.title: BICLA response at week 52 (key secondary; primary SRI-4 missed)
SRI-4 response at week 52 (pre-specified primary, NOT met)

NCT02446912.snippet: Source: Furie R et al. Lancet Rheumatol 2019;1:e208-e219 (TULIP-1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 457 adults with moderate-severe active SLE (SLEDAI-2K >=6, BILAG grade A or B) on stable background therapy randomized double-blind to anifrolumab 300 mg IV every 4 weeks (n=180), anifrolumab 150 mg (n=93), or placebo (n=184) for 52 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - SRI-4 (NOT MET)
evidence.text: Pre-specified primary endpoint SRI-4 response at week 52: anifrolumab 300 mg 65/180 (36.2%) vs placebo 49/184 (40.0%); treatment difference -3.8 pct points (95% CI -13.6 to +6.0; P=0.41). Trial DID NOT meet the SRI-4 primary.

evidence.label: Key Secondary - BICLA
evidence.text: BICLA response at week 52: anifrolumab 122/180 (67.6%) vs placebo 92/184 (50.0%); odds ratio 1.55 (95% CI 1.03 to 2.33). BICLA was a pre-specified key secondary that became the regulatory primary for TULIP-2 after TULIP-1 SRI-4 miss.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 91% completion. D4 SOME CONCERNS - primary SRI-4 endpoint not met; BICLA became pre-specified primary in TULIP-2, which may represent outcome-switching across the two-trial programme. Transparent in reporting. D5 LOW.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 70 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. NCT02446899.name: TULIP-2
NCT02446899.group: Moderate-severe SLE on standard therapy, anifrolumab 300 mg IV Q4W vs placebo (wk 52, BICLA primary)
NCT02446899.snippet: Source: Morand EF et al. N Engl J Med 2020;382:211-221 (TULIP-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 362 adults with moderate-severe active SLE on stable background therapy randomized 1:1 double-blind to anifrolumab 300 mg IV every 4 weeks (n=180) or placebo (n=182) for 52 weeks. BICLA was the pre-specified primary endpoint (modification from TULIP-1 programme after SRI-4 miss).

evidence.label: Primary - BICLA at Week 52
evidence.text: BICLA response at week 52: anifrolumab 86/180 (47.8%) vs placebo 57/182 (31.5%); treatment difference 16.3 pct points (95% CI 6.3 to 26.3; P=0.001). Odds ratio 1.99 (95% CI 1.31 to 3.03). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Glucocorticoid Sparing
evidence.text: Sustained oral glucocorticoid reduction to <=7.5 mg/d prednisone from wk 40-52 in patients receiving >=10 mg/d at baseline: anifrolumab 51.5% vs placebo 30.2%; OR 1.77 (95% CI 1.16-2.70). CLASI-50 skin response higher with anifrolumab.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization stratified by SLEDAI-2K, type-I IFN signature, region. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 93% completion. D4 LOW - BICLA centrally adjudicated. D5 LOW - SAP locked (BICLA primary pre-specified after TULIP-1 read-out; outcome-switching transparently reported).
